message:
Simplify DecodingText from rAF scramble to Framer Motion fade-in

Completely replaces the requestAnimationFrame-based character
scramble animation with a minimal Framer Motion entrance effect.

Removed:
- The entire rAF animation loop (startTimeRef, frameRef, animate
  callback) that progressively revealed characters left-to-right
  by replacing unresolved positions with random digits
- The hover-triggered re-scramble (500ms random digit burst on
  mouseEnter)
- The invisible "ghost" span layout technique that reserved space
  with an opacity-0 duplicate of the final text
- The tabular-nums / whitespace-pre CSS for monospace digit alignment
- All state management (displayText, isDone)
- The "0123456789" character set constant

Added:
- A single motion.span with opacity 0→1 and y 10→0, using a
  custom cubic-bezier ease [0.16, 1, 0.3, 1] for a snappy
  entrance feel
- The text is rendered directly as children, eliminating the
  dual-span absolute positioning trick entirely

The scramble effect, while visually interesting, caused layout
instability on slower devices and added unnecessary JavaScript
weight to every page heading. The simpler fade-in is more
reliable across all viewport sizes and requires zero state.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
